bullets.short: Leading full build-out of Rho's 325 Pacific SF headquarters, coordinating 15+ vendors across 3 concurrent floors from demolition through move-in
bullets.detail: Managing a complete multi-floor renovation with no general contractor — all vendor sequencing, dependency management, and critical-path decisions owned directly. A structural blocker on Floor 3 mid-project made the planned electrical approach infeasible; researched and proposed a surface-mounted Connect Track alternative that preserved the timeline. Running weekly construction syncs with brokers, designers, and contractors while tracking all workstreams in a Notion system with Granola integration. Project on track for substantial completion week of May 4, 2026, with no timeline slippage attributable to coordination failure.

bullets.short: Identified and disputed a $9K+ electrical overquote and design firm billing discrepancies through systematic invoice auditing
bullets.detail: With 15+ active vendors and no dedicated finance oversight, invoice accuracy was entirely self-managed across a high-volume project cycle. Cross-referenced the design firm's billed meeting hours against Google Calendar records to surface a discrepancy, and flagged a separate ~$9K electrical overquote by pushing for pricing transparency. Also identified an additional ~$5K savings opportunity by relocating flat mold electrical from ceiling to floor on Floor 3. Both issues escalated with documentation rather than absorbed into the budget.

bullets.short: Resolved a 44-fixture lighting crisis and a historic district permit rejection in the same project week without impacting the construction timeline
bullets.detail: Alcon shipped pendant fixtures instead of the specified surface-mount configuration, threatening both the install schedule and UL certification. Diagnosed the root cause with the vendor and electrician, then scoped a two-part remediation using aircraft cables and a 3-wire cable roll that preserved certification at $0 incremental cost. Simultaneously, the city rejected the exterior signage permit due to historic district restrictions — pivoted to a no-permit acrylic panel replacement that required no new electrical and unblocked the scope entirely. Both blockers closed within the same week.

bullets.short: Delivered $30K under budget on office design initiative by sourcing and managing local vendor partnerships
bullets.detail: Led the vendor strategy for a full office design initiative, sourcing local partnerships as an alternative to higher-cost default vendors. Managed the vendor relationships end-to-end — from scoping and negotiation through delivery — while maintaining design quality and timeline. Result: came in $30K under the approved budget without scope reduction.

bullets.short: Led redesign and operational strategy for SF hub event space, improving usability for internal and client-facing programming
bullets.detail: Assessed the existing event space layout and identified friction points limiting its use for both internal team programming and external client events. Drove the redesign from operational and experiential angles — layout, AV capability, and workflow — to make the space more flexible and high-impact. The updated space improved usability across both programming types without requiring a full build-out.

bullets.short: Built and implemented internal service catalog in Freshworks, creating scalable workflows for workplace and facilities requests
bullets.detail: Identified that incoming workplace and facilities requests lacked a consistent intake and routing process, creating response delays and dropped items. Built a structured service catalog in Freshworks that standardized request categories, routing logic, and SLAs across the team. The system created a scalable, trackable workflow that reduced manual triage and improved response consistency.

bullets.short: Launched workplace program that increased in-office attendance by 35% using engagement insights to inform approach
bullets.detail: Identified a decline in in-office attendance and used engagement data to understand the underlying drivers before designing a response. Built and launched a workplace program targeting the specific friction points — not a generic events calendar — grounded in what the data showed employees actually valued. The program drove a 35% increase in in-office attendance.

bullets.short: Owned daily workplace operations supporting CFO, HR, and Development leadership as the central coordination point across departments
bullets.detail: Served as the operational hub for executive-level stakeholders across three departments, managing competing priorities and requests without a formal escalation structure. Maintained consistent facility performance and service delivery across the organization by owning vendor relationships, service scheduling, and issue resolution directly. This role required independent judgment daily — knowing when to solve, escalate, or push back.

bullets.short: Led onboarding and offboarding operations including access control, hardware provisioning, and workspace readiness
bullets.detail_needed: Would benefit from: volume of onboardings managed, any process improvements made, or a specific example of a complex onboarding/offboarding case handled.

bullets.short: Implemented digital signage system (Optisign), reducing annual costs by $15K while improving workplace communication
bullets.detail: Evaluated the existing signage approach and identified an opportunity to replace it with a more cost-effective and flexible digital system. Selected and implemented Optisign across the sites, managing the rollout from vendor selection through deployment. The switch reduced annual costs by $15K while improving the quality and responsiveness of workplace communication.

bullets.short: Leveraged Envoy utilization data to inform space planning and operational decisions across multiple sites
bullets.detail: Used Envoy occupancy and utilization data to move space planning decisions from intuition to evidence — identifying underused areas, peak usage patterns, and mismatches between allocated and actual space use. Translated the data into actionable operational recommendations rather than leaving it in dashboards. This approach informed layout adjustments and resource allocation across multiple sites.

bullets.short: Drove member retention to 3.5% churn through proactive engagement strategies and community programming
bullets.detail: Managed member relationships in a high-volume coworking environment where churn was a direct business metric. Built proactive touchpoints — community events, check-ins, and programming — designed to surface dissatisfaction before it became a cancellation. Churn held at 3.5%, reflecting a retention-first approach to member experience.

bullets.short: Managed front-of-house operations, logistics, and member services in a high-volume coworking environment
bullets.detail_needed: Would benefit from: volume figures (members served, daily traffic), a specific operational challenge overcome, or a process improvement implemented.

bullets.short: Led office operations, vendor coordination, and onboarding processes for internal teams
bullets.detail_needed: Need more context: team size supported, vendor types managed, any notable process improvements, or a specific operational challenge resolved.
